questions.text: Pearl is mainly constituted of
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Calcium Carbonate**

Pearls are mainly composed of **calcium carbonate (CaCO3)** arranged in crystalline layers.

Option Analysis:
• Calcium Oxalate → Found in some kidney stones.
• Calcium Sulphate → Found in gypsum and plaster of Paris.
• **Calcium Carbonate → Correct. Major component of pearls and shells.**
• Calcium Oxide → Quicklime.

questions.text: What are Rubies and Sapphires chemically known as?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Aluminium Oxide**

Rubies and sapphires are varieties of **corundum (Al2O3)**.

Color differences occur due to trace impurities:
• Ruby → Chromium
• Sapphire → Iron or titanium.

questions.text: These days yellow lamps are frequently used as street light. Which one of the following is used in these lamps?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Sodium**

Street lights commonly use **sodium vapour lamps** which emit bright yellow light.

Advantages:
• High luminous efficiency
• Good visibility in fog.

questions.text: Sodium Vapour Lamp is usually used as street light, because
options: Light from this is monochromatic and will not split through water droplets
It is pleasing to the eyes
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Monochromatic light**

Sodium lamps produce nearly **single wavelength yellow light**, reducing scattering in fog and rain.

questions.text: The most commonly used substance in fluorescent tubes are
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Mercury Vapour and Argon**

Fluorescent lamps work by passing electricity through **mercury vapour mixed with argon gas**.

questions.text: Which one of the following metals is accessed in the native state?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Gold**

Gold occurs naturally in **pure metallic form** because it is chemically less reactive.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of the given substances in the decreasing order of their densities?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Gold > Mercury > Steel**

Approx densities:
• Gold ≈ 19.3 g/cm³
• Mercury ≈ 13.6 g/cm³
• Steel ≈ 7.8 g/cm³

questions.text: Of how many carats is the pure gold?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 24 carat**

Carat indicates **purity of gold**.

Examples:
• 24 carat → Pure gold
• 22 carat → Jewelry gold

questions.text: The softest mineral, Talc (Soapstone) is mainly
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Magnesium Silicate**

Talc formula: **Mg3Si4O10(OH)2**.

It is the **softest mineral on Mohs hardness scale (1)**.

questions.text: The chemical name of limestone is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Calcium Carbonate**

Limestone consists mainly of **CaCO3**.

questions.text: Chemically ‘Plaster of Paris’ is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Calcium Sulphate**

Plaster of Paris = **Calcium sulphate hemihydrate (CaSO4·½H2O)**.

questions.text: The chemical formula of the Plaster of Paris is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: CaSO4·½H2O**

Plaster of Paris is produced by heating **gypsum (CaSO4·2H2O)**.

questions.text: Doctors, Artists and Sculptors use Calcium Sulphate which is popularly known as
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Plaster of Paris**

Used for:
• Medical casts
• Sculptures
• Decorative moulds.

questions.text: Monazite is an ore of
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Thorium**

Monazite sands contain **thorium phosphate**.

questions.text: Mica is a
options: Good conductor of heat and bad conductor of electricity
Bad conductor of both heat and electricity
Good conductor of heat and electricity both
Bad conductor of heat and good conductor of electricity
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Good conductor of heat but poor conductor of electricity**.

Mica is used as **electrical insulator** in electrical appliances.

questions.text: In which of the following industries is mica used as a raw material?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Electrical industry**.

Mica is used for:
• Electrical insulation
• Capacitors
• Electronic devices.

questions.text: Which one of the following liquids is very good conductor of heat?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Mercury**.

Mercury is a **metallic liquid**, so it conducts heat efficiently.

questions.text: Which one of the following metals is liquid at room temperature?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Mercury**.

Mercury is the only metal **liquid at room temperature (~25°C)**.

questions.text: Which one of the following metals is liquid at ordinary temperature?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Mercury**.

Again confirms mercury as **liquid metal under normal conditions**.

questions.text: Mercury is basically used in thermometer devices because its especiality is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: High circulation power (high thermal expansion)**.

Mercury expands uniformly with temperature, making it ideal for thermometers.
